MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PTR-09-292890 The Robert Yee Trust.

Appearances: Bruce Atwater for the Szeto Brothers.

Mr. Atwater stated that the elder abuse claim was filed in unlimited jusridiction in San Francisco, that there was no movement regarding resolution of the will contest and the 850 petition, that there was very little contact between Counsel and that he will be asking for the matters to be consolidated so that they can be heard together as the issues and the Parties overlap. In addition, Mr. Atwater stated that no answer was filed in the elder abuse matter and that new Counsel has been retained. The Court will put the matter over for a period of time when the civil case is at issue and then send both matters to a settlement conference. The matters were continued to September 27, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of whether the civil action is at issue and possible referal to a settlement conference in both actions.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Atwater, Esq. for Petitioners Nelson & Tony Szeto. Ed Liu, Esq. for Respondent Ann Chan.

The Court stated to counsel that the court is ready to send this matter out to trial. Mr. Liu stated that trial counsel is in Europe until the New Year, and there were depositions and some pending discovery, and requested a 90 days continuance. Mr. Atwater stated that he is not objecting to the request. The Court stated that matter has been sitting for two years and 90 days is too long; discovery issue has been delayed since January. The Court continued the matter to Feb. 1, 2011 at 9:00 am. in Dept. 204 for Trial assignment.

MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 292890 The Robert Wing Yee Trust.

Appearances: Norman Lew for Ann Chan. Tom Peraic for Nelson Szeto and Tony Szeto.

Mr. Lew stated that the Estate of Fred Ong mediated prior to filing of the Yee Trust and suggested further mediation. Mr. Peraic stated that he had no objection to further mediation, that he felt it was proper to consolidate the actions and that there was no petition for consolidation. In addition, Mr. Peraic requested a trial date be set, suggested an evidentiary hearing on the Trust matter, stated that the probate matter may become moot and that both matters would take about four days for trial. The Court stated that discovery should be conducted now and suggested Counsel meet and confer about outstanding discovery. The matters were continued to May 12, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and for referral to the Department of the Presiding Judge for trial setting. The request to consolidate the matters was granted. Mr. Peraic is to prepare the order.

Off Calendar Nov-25-2009 Continued To Jan-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Norman Lew, Esq. for Respondent/surviving spouse Ann Chan, Charlene Johnson, Esq. for Petitioners Nelson and Tony Szeto.
Mr. Lew reported that he is waiting for the return of the signed document and requested to put over the matter for 60 days to complete the discovery.
Ms. Johnson stated that she did not have any good news.
Matter was continued to Jan. 27, 2010 for status on the settlement or to set for trial date.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Off Calendar Oct-07-2009 Continued To Nov-25-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Norman Lew for Ann Chan (Respondent/Petitioner). Charlene Johnson for Nelson Szeto and Tony Szeto.

Mr. Lew requested a continuance stating that they had attended mediation, that they were provided with information that may or may not resolve the matter and that it was being considered by his office. Ms. Johnson concurred and informed the Court that the issue regarding the order appointing the Special Administrator and the issuance of letters had been resolved. The matter was continued to November 25, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Carl Sauceda, CSR#2031. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-05-2009 Continued To Oct-07-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charlene Johnson for Nelson Szeto and Tony Szeto.

Ms. Johnson stated that Norman Lew did not appear due to a conflict, that they agreed to participate in mediation and that they agreed on an Administrator. The Court signed the order referring the matter to pro bono mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than September 30, 2009. The matters were continued to October 7, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on mediation.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-27-2009 Continued To Jul-08-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Norman Lew for Ann Chan. Lucas Munoz for Nelson Szeto and Tony Szeto (Contestants).

Mr. Lew stated that discovery had been propounded by Mr. Munoz and that they may be able to settle the matter. Mr. Munoz stated that he needed some written discovery, that the need for a neutral Special Administrator would not change, that there was concern that the assets of the estate would dissipate and that his Client believed that there are additional cash assets. The matter was continued to July 8, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the Parties to reach some accord as to who the Special Administrator should be. The Parties will meet and confer.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-20-2009 Continued To May-27-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Norman Lew for Ann Chan (Respondent to will contest). Scott Bassin for Ann Chan (Petitioner to be appointed as Executor). Lucas Munoz for Tony Szeto and Nelson Szeto.

The Parties presented argument to support their positions with regard to standing, which will the Court should consider admitting, undue influence and the appointment of a Special Administator. Mr. Munoz stated that he intended to conduct discovery. Mr. Lew advised the Court that the only asset in the estate was a 1/9th interest in a property located on Broadway Street. The matters were continued to May 27, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel was directed to file their petitions for Appointment of Special Administrator with a hearing date of May 27, 2009 as well. Participation in pro bono mediation will be discussed at that time.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
